description: Extrato obrigatorio no onboarding/upgrade para Conta Azul. Upload de CSV 'Dinheiro em conta' do MP como requisito para ativacao com CA, com validacao de cobertura, ingestao, GDrive backup, e aba de gestao no admin.

userStories.title: Migration: add extrato_missing to sellers
userStories.description: As a system, I need new columns on the sellers table to track whether a seller was activated without an extrato CSV.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration 010_extrato_missing.sql in migrations/
Add column extrato_missing BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE to sellers table
Add column extrato_uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL to sellers table
Migration uses IF NOT EXISTS / ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Pattern: see migrations/007_extrato_uploads.sql for style. The extrato_uploads table already exists — this story only adds columns to the sellers table.

userStories.title: Backend: validate_extrato_coverage() service function
userStories.description: As a system, I need a function that validates whether multiple extrato CSVs together cover the full period from ca_start_date to yesterday, detecting gaps.
acceptanceCriteria: New function validate_extrato_coverage(csv_texts: list[str], ca_start_date: str) -> dict in app/services/extrato_ingester.py
Uses existing _parse_account_statement() to extract dates from each CSV
Returns dict with keys: valid (bool), covered_months (list[str]), missing_months (list[str]), gaps (list[dict with start/end]), min_date, max_date, error (str|None)
Rejects if min(dates) > ca_start_date with clear error message
Rejects if max(dates) < yesterday with clear error message
Detects internal gaps between CSVs (e.g. jan+mar without feb) and reports them
Accepts overlapping CSVs (e.g. jan+jan is OK, dedup is handled by ingester)
Validates CSV format — rejects if INITIAL_BALANCE header is missing
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Key context: _parse_account_statement() is at line ~245 in extrato_ingester.py. It returns (summary, transactions) where each transaction has a 'date' field in YYYY-MM-DD format. The CSV format has RELEASE_DATE;TRANSACTION_TYPE;REFERENCE_ID;TRANSACTION_NET_AMOUNT;PARTIAL_BALANCE with dates as DD-MM-YYYY. See sample file at testes/data/extratos/extrato fevereiro 141Air.csv. Tolerance: gaps of up to 1 day are acceptable (weekends without movements). Coverage validation: the function checks that the period ca_start_date to yesterday-BRT is fully covered by the union of all CSV date ranges.

userStories.title: Backend: multi-file extrato upload endpoint
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to upload multiple extrato CSVs for a seller at once, with coverage validation and GDrive backup.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint POST /admin/sellers/{slug}/extrato/upload in app/routers/admin/extrato.py
Accepts multiple files via UploadFile (files: list[UploadFile])
Requires seller to have integration_mode=dashboard_ca and ca_start_date set
Calls validate_extrato_coverage() from US-002 to validate date coverage
Returns 422 with clear error if coverage validation fails (gaps, missing months, invalid format)
On validation pass: calls ingest_extrato_from_csv() for each CSV (one per detected month)
Records each processed month in extrato_uploads table (existing table, upsert by seller_slug+month)
Uploads each CSV to Google Drive in background: ROOT/EXTRATOS/{SELLER_NAME}/{YYYY-MM}.csv using gdrive_client.py pattern
Updates sellers.extrato_missing=false and sellers.extrato_uploaded_at=now() on success
Returns aggregate stats: total files, total lines ingested, total errors, months processed, gdrive_status
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Key files to reference: app/routers/admin/extrato.py (existing upload_extrato endpoint at line 125 — single file per month, use as pattern but create NEW endpoint for multi-file), app/services/extrato_ingester.py (ingest_extrato_from_csv at line 982), app/services/gdrive_client.py (upload_expenses_zip pattern — reuse _build_gdrive_client, _gdrive_ensure_folder, _gdrive_upload_bytes). The existing upload_extrato accepts single file+month via Form fields. The new endpoint should auto-detect months from CSV content. GDrive folder structure: ROOT/EXTRATOS/{seller.dashboard_empresa or seller.name}/{YYYY-MM}.csv. GDrive upload must run in background via asyncio.create_task(asyncio.to_thread(...)) to not block the response. If no LEGACY_DAILY_GOOGLE_DRIVE_ROOT_FOLDER_ID is configured, skip GDrive silently.

userStories.title: Backend: modify activate/upgrade to support extrato_missing flag
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the activate and upgrade endpoints to accept a skip_extrato flag so I can activate a seller without extrato when needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Add optional field skip_extrato: bool = False to ActivateSellerRequest in app/routers/admin/sellers.py
When integration_mode=dashboard_ca and skip_extrato=True: set sellers.extrato_missing=True in the update payload
When integration_mode=dashboard_ca and skip_extrato=False (default): set sellers.extrato_missing=False (extrato upload expected separately)
Apply same logic to upgrade_seller_to_ca endpoint (around line 300 in sellers.py)
No change to backfill trigger — backfill still starts immediately regardless of extrato status
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Key file: app/routers/admin/sellers.py. ActivateSellerRequest is at line 168. activate_seller_v2 is at line 179. upgrade_seller_to_ca is around line 295. The backfill is triggered via asyncio.create_task(run_onboarding_backfill(slug)) — do NOT change this. The extrato upload happens separately via US-003 endpoint (before or after activation).

userStories.title: Backend: modify onboarding_backfill to log extrato status
userStories.description: As a system, the onboarding backfill should check and log the extrato status between release report and baixas steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Add new step in _execute_backfill() in app/services/onboarding_backfill.py between release report backfill (step 6b) and baixas (step 7)
New step queries extrato_uploads table for this seller to find uploads with status=completed
If uploads exist: logs 'extrato already ingested via upload, N months covered'
If no uploads and seller has extrato_missing=True: logs 'extrato ingestion skipped (extrato_missing=true)'
If no uploads and extrato_missing=False: logs warning 'no extrato uploaded yet, coverage may be incomplete'
Progress dict includes extrato_status key with value 'covered' or 'missing' or 'pending'
Step is non-fatal: any exception is caught and logged as warning without aborting backfill
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Key file: app/services/onboarding_backfill.py. The _execute_backfill() function is at line 266. Release report backfill is step 6b (lines 421-447). Baixas is step 7 (lines 449-469). Insert the new step between them. The actual ingestion of extrato CSVs happens at upload time (US-003), NOT during backfill. The backfill step just checks status and logs.

userStories.title: Backend: extrato coverage status endpoint for admin dashboard
userStories.description: As an admin, I need an endpoint that returns the extrato coverage status for all dashboard_ca sellers.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint GET /admin/extrato/sellers-status in app/routers/admin/extrato.py
Returns list of sellers with integration_mode=dashboard_ca
Each seller entry includes: slug, name, dashboard_empresa, ca_start_date, extrato_missing, extrato_uploaded_at
Each seller entry includes months_needed: list of YYYY-MM strings from ca_start_date to current month
Each seller entry includes months_uploaded: list of YYYY-MM strings from extrato_uploads table where status=completed
Each seller entry includes months_missing: months_needed minus months_uploaded
Each seller entry includes coverage_status: 'complete' or 'partial' or 'missing'
Endpoint requires admin auth (Depends(require_admin))
python3 -m pytest passes
userStories.notes: Key file: app/routers/admin/extrato.py. To list sellers: db.table('sellers').select('slug, name, dashboard_empresa, ca_start_date, extrato_missing, extrato_uploaded_at, integration_mode, active').eq('integration_mode', 'dashboard_ca').eq('active', True). For months_needed: generate YYYY-MM list from ca_start_date to current month. For months_uploaded: db.table('extrato_uploads').select('seller_slug, month').eq('status', 'completed') then group by seller_slug.

userStories.title: Frontend: useExtrato hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a React hook for extrato API calls (coverage status, multi-file upload, upload history).
acceptanceCriteria: New file dashboard/src/hooks/useExtrato.ts
Function loadSellersStatus(): fetches GET /admin/extrato/sellers-status, returns typed array
Function uploadExtratos(sellerSlug: string, files: File[]): POSTs to /admin/sellers/{slug}/extrato/upload as multipart/form-data with multiple files
Function loadUploadHistory(sellerSlug: string): fetches GET /admin/extrato/uploads/{slug}, returns typed array (extracts .data from envelope)
All functions include X-Admin-Token header from sessionStorage.getItem('adminToken')
All functions handle 401 by calling onUnauthorized callback
TypeScript types exported: ExtratoSellerStatus, ExtratoUploadResult, ExtratoUploadRecord
Typecheck passes (cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: Pattern: follow dashboard/src/hooks/useExpenses.ts exactly for structure. It receives { onUnauthorized } and uses fetch() with X-Admin-Token header. The admin API base URL pattern: in production use relative URLs (/admin/...), in dev use the same origin. ExtratoSellerStatus type should have: slug, name, dashboard_empresa, ca_start_date, extrato_missing, extrato_uploaded_at, months_needed, months_uploaded, months_missing, coverage_status. ExtratoUploadRecord type: id, seller_slug, month, filename, status, lines_total, lines_ingested, lines_skipped, lines_already_covered, initial_balance, final_balance, uploaded_at.

userStories.title: Frontend: ExtratoTab component with seller cards
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a tab showing cards per seller with extrato coverage status and multi-file upload capability.
acceptanceCriteria: New file dashboard/src/components/ExtratoTab.tsx with CSS module dashboard/src/components/ExtratoTab.module.css
Component receives sellers[] and onLogout() props (same interface as ExpensesExportTab)
Shows grid of cards for sellers with integration_mode=dashboard_ca (sorted alphabetically by dashboard_empresa or name)
Each card shows: seller name, ca_start_date, coverage_status badge (complete=green, partial=yellow, missing=red)
Each card shows list of months with OK or MISSING indicator per month
Each card has upload button that opens file picker (accepts multiple .csv files)
Upload shows inline success/error result on the card
Card with extrato_missing=true shows 'Sem extrato' warning badge
Upload history per seller is collapsible (same expand/collapse pattern as ExpensesExportTab batch history)
Upload history shows: month, filename, lines_ingested, status, uploaded_at
Sellers without integration_mode=dashboard_ca are not shown
Typecheck passes (cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: Pattern: closely follow dashboard/src/components/ExpensesExportTab.tsx for card grid layout (it uses activeSellers sorted alphabetically, Map<string, State> for per-seller state, Set<string> for expandedSlugs). CSS Modules only (no Tailwind). Design tokens in dashboard/src/index.css: --success (#10b981), --attention (#d97706), --danger (#ef4444). File input: accept='.csv,text/csv', multiple=true. Use the useExtrato hook from US-007. Filter sellers by checking if they appear in the sellersStatus response (only dashboard_ca sellers will be there).

userStories.title: Frontend: add Extratos tab to AdminPanel
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a third tab 'Extratos' in the admin panel alongside 'Sellers' and 'Despesas'.
acceptanceCriteria: AdminPanel.tsx has a third tab option 'Extratos'
Tab renders ExtratoTab component when selected
ExtratoTab receives sellers array and onLogout callback (same props as ExpensesExportTab)
Tab order: Sellers | Despesas | Extratos
Typecheck passes (cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: Key file: dashboard/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx. It currently has tab switching logic between 'sellers' and 'expenses'. Add 'extratos' as a third tab value. Import ExtratoTab from ./ExtratoTab. Follow the exact same conditional rendering pattern used for ExpensesExportTab. The tab buttons are in the header area of AdminPanel.
